LANDING_HERO.HEADLINE: Track meals, plan ahead, and see what actually works.
LANDING_HERO.SUBTITLE: Food Diary combines meal logging, nutrition planning, progress analytics, focused routines, and specialist collaboration in one product workspace.

SEARCH.TITLE: For people looking for a food diary app, calorie counter, and practical nutrition planner
SEARCH.TEXT: Keep an online food diary, log breakfast, lunch, dinner, and snacks, track calories and macros, plan weekly meals, and review how nutrition connects with weight, waist, fasting, and long-term habits.

LANDING_HERO.REASSURANCE: Free to start • No ads • Built for long-term routines

PANEL.TITLE: Nutrition in one place
PANEL.DESCRIPTION: Food Diary brings tracking, planning, analytics, and collaboration into one workflow.
PANEL.NOTE_TEXT: Entries, plans, and progress stay in one system.

MEALS_PREVIEW.TITLE: Today's meals preview
MEALS_PREVIEW.PLACEHOLDER: Add a meal to see it here

FEATURES.TITLE: More than a calorie diary
FEATURES.SUBTITLE: Food Diary already covers planning, tracking, progress, and guided routines, so the landing should show the real breadth of the product.

SUMMARY.TITLE: A nutrition workspace, not a single-purpose tracker
SUMMARY.TEXT: Switch between categories to preview how Food Diary supports daily logging, planning, body metrics, focused programs, and long-term motivation.

TRACK.LABEL: Track
TRACK.TITLE: Capture meals without losing context
TRACK.DESCRIPTION: Products, recipes, and meal logs work together so you can record what you ate and keep reusable building blocks close.

PLAN.LABEL: Plan
PLAN.TITLE: Build routines before the day gets busy
PLAN.DESCRIPTION: Plan meals, organize ingredients, and connect nutrition goals to the choices you make during the week.

PROGRESS.LABEL: Progress
PROGRESS.TITLE: See trends, not isolated numbers
PROGRESS.DESCRIPTION: Charts and check-ins turn raw entries into feedback loops, so it is easier to notice direction and stay consistent.

SPECIAL.LABEL: Programs
SPECIAL.TITLE: Support more specific health workflows
SPECIAL.DESCRIPTION: Use dedicated tools for fasting, cycle tracking, and premium AI-assisted flows when your routine needs more structure.

MOTIVATION.LABEL: Motivation
MOTIVATION.TITLE: Stay engaged after the first week
MOTIVATION.DESCRIPTION: Lessons, gamified progress, and account sync help Food Diary feel like an evolving system instead of a static logbook.

LOG_MEALS.TITLE: Meal logging with reusable entries
LOG_MEALS.DESCRIPTION: Track breakfasts, lunches, dinners, and snacks while keeping nutrition totals visible across the day.

PRODUCT_LIBRARY.TITLE: Your own product library
PRODUCT_LIBRARY.DESCRIPTION: Save favorite foods and quickly add them again instead of rebuilding the same entries every time.

RECIPE_FLOW.TITLE: Recipes that fit the diary flow
RECIPE_FLOW.DESCRIPTION: Store recipes as structured items and drop them into meals with nutrition already connected.

MEAL_PLANS.TITLE: Plan your days in advance
MEAL_PLANS.DESCRIPTION: Use meal plans to outline future meals and reduce decision fatigue before hunger drives the choice.

SHOPPING_LISTS.TITLE: Turn plans into shopping lists
SHOPPING_LISTS.DESCRIPTION: Move from planned meals to ingredient lists so execution is easier outside the app too.

GOALS.TITLE: Keep targets connected to behavior
GOALS.DESCRIPTION: Tie calorie and nutrition targets to actual routines instead of treating them like static numbers.

STATISTICS.TITLE: Visual nutrition analytics
STATISTICS.DESCRIPTION: See calorie, macro, and distribution charts that make weekly patterns easier to understand.

BODY_HISTORY.TITLE: Weight and waist trends
BODY_HISTORY.DESCRIPTION: Track body changes alongside food data to compare behavior with longer-term physical outcomes.

WEEKLY_CHECKINS.TITLE: Weekly check-ins and reflection
WEEKLY_CHECKINS.DESCRIPTION: Step back from daily logging and review consistency, changes, and next adjustments.

FASTING.TITLE: Dedicated fasting workflow
FASTING.DESCRIPTION: Run fasting timers and supporting screens without forcing the feature into the standard meal log.

CYCLE_TRACKING.TITLE: Cycle-aware tracking
CYCLE_TRACKING.DESCRIPTION: Track cycle data in the same product so nutrition, body signals, and timing are easier to compare.

PREMIUM_AI.TITLE: Premium and AI-assisted tools
PREMIUM_AI.DESCRIPTION: Unlock advanced features and guided flows when you need more automation or deeper support.

LESSONS.TITLE: Built-in learning content
LESSONS.DESCRIPTION: Use lessons to turn the app into an ongoing education tool, not just a place to store entries.

GAMIFICATION.TITLE: Progress that feels rewarding
GAMIFICATION.DESCRIPTION: Streaks and game-like progress cues can help users stay engaged between visible body changes.

PROFILE_SYNC.TITLE: A synced personal workspace
PROFILE_SYNC.DESCRIPTION: Keep your profile, history, and progress together so the system grows more useful over time.

LANDING_DIETOLOGIST.TITLE: Work with a specialist in the same space as your diary
LANDING_DIETOLOGIST.SUBTITLE: Invite a dietologist by email, share only the categories you choose, and keep meals, progress, and body metrics in one workflow instead of sending manual reports.

INVITE.TITLE: Invite by email
INVITE.TEXT: Connect a dietologist from your profile without moving the conversation into separate spreadsheets or chat threads.

SHARE.TITLE: Share selected diary data
SHARE.TEXT: Meals, nutrition statistics, weight, waist, goals, hydration, and fasting access can be enabled only when you want them visible.

ADJUST.TITLE: Keep control over access
ADJUST.TEXT: Update permissions later, cancel a pending invite, or disconnect the relationship if your workflow changes.

CARD.TITLE: Dietologist collaboration, with scoped access
CARD.TEXT: The dietologist flow is built around explicit permissions, so shared care can happen inside the app without exposing everything by default.

LANDING_DIETOLOGIST.NOTE: You decide which data categories are visible to the dietologist, and can revise access later.

PREVIEW.TITLE: See your day at a glance
PREVIEW.SUBTITLE: Charts, meals, and trends live together so you always know where you stand.

MEALS.TITLE: Preview today in one list
MEALS.SUBTITLE: Add products and recipes to sketch your day before you log.

SUMMARY.TITLE: Daily totals stay visible
SUMMARY.SUBTITLE: Calories and macro balance update as you build a plan.

NUTRITION.TITLE: Tune your macros
NUTRITION.SUBTITLE: Trends and distributions show how protein, fats, and carbs split.

BODY.TITLE: Follow body metrics
BODY.SUBTITLE: Weight and waist charts highlight long-term progress.

TOUR.TITLE: Products, recipes and quick add
TOUR.SUBTITLE: Pick items you eat often and drop them into a quick drawer before logging a meal.
TOUR.CATALOG_TITLE: Build a meal from familiar products and recipes
TOUR.CATALOG_SUBTITLE: Keep your usual cards close so you can sketch a future meal quickly instead of bouncing between separate screens.
TOUR.DRAFT_TITLE: Assemble the meal first, save it in one step later
TOUR.DRAFT_SUBTITLE: Add products and recipes into one shared draft, adjust the mix before logging, and keep the final meal in one clear flow.
TOUR.DRAFT_PILL_ONE: Products and recipes in one draft
TOUR.DRAFT_PILL_TWO: Reuse common items without manual re-entry
TOUR.DRAFT_NOTE: This makes it easier to sketch routine breakfasts, snacks, and day-ahead plans without losing context between the library and the diary.
TOUR.DRAWER_HINT: Preview: edit and save actions unlock after you sign up.

LANDING_STEPS.TITLE: Three steps to a clear routine
LANDING_STEPS.SUBTITLE: From sign-up to tracking in seconds.

STEP1.TITLE: Create an account
STEP1.TEXT: Answer a few questions so we can tailor your diary.

STEP2.TITLE: Log meals with products or recipes
STEP2.TEXT: Add portions, scan items, and reuse saved meals.

STEP3.TITLE: Watch your progress
STEP3.TEXT: Track calories, macros, weight and trends in one place.

LANDING_CTA.TITLE: Start with your diary. Grow into your full nutrition workspace.
LANDING_CTA.SUBTITLE: Create an account to log meals, plan ahead, review progress, and unlock dietologist collaboration when you need it.

LANDING_FAQ.TITLE: Questions before you start
LANDING_FAQ.SUBTITLE: Short answers about what Food Diary already covers and how it fits into a long-term nutrition routine.
LANDING_FAQ.DETAIL_LINK: Read the detailed Food Diary page
LANDING_FAQ.GUIDES_TITLE: Explore dedicated pages for key nutrition use cases

APP_SCOPE.QUESTION: Is Food Diary only a calorie counter?
APP_SCOPE.ANSWER: No. Food Diary combines meal logging, calorie and macro tracking, recipes, meal planning, fasting tools, body metrics, weekly reviews, and guided nutrition workflows in one workspace.

PLANNING.QUESTION: Can I plan meals in advance?
PLANNING.ANSWER: Yes. You can build meal plans, turn planned meals into shopping lists, and keep products and recipes ready so future meals are easier to assemble.

PROGRESS.QUESTION: Can I track long-term progress, not just daily entries?
PROGRESS.ANSWER: Yes. Food Diary includes charts for calories and macros, body progress such as weight and waist history, and weekly check-ins so you can review trends over time.

DIETOLOGIST.QUESTION: Can I work with a dietologist inside the app?
DIETOLOGIST.ANSWER: Yes. You can invite a dietologist by email, share only the data categories you choose, and keep meals, progress, and body metrics in the same workflow.
